4.narrative: The Fearsome Fivesome The Bastards of Bogtown represent the scum that seems The five are led by the elf Findalu. They are a mixed to wander into the region each year. The Bastards consist group and possess little loyalty to each other. They of thieves, knaves, and cut-throats. Their strength rests have experience battling froglings near Bogtown and in their numbers. They are led by Ferwig the Frenetic, have worked for the Silver Standard Caravan Company a wizard of note. They prefer to waylay adventuring on occasion. parties as they return from the Barrowmoor in the hopes of finding them wounded, slow, and laden with treasure. 206

1.narrative: Nergal’s Beckoning: This power is a stronger, more Tablet of Chaos withers and dies. powerful, mass-effect form of the spell Animate Dead. Nergal’s Beckoning animates the dead and they remain Destroying The Tablet of Chaos animated until destroyed. Unlike the spell Animate The Tablet is impervious to spells, physical attacks, Dead, which limits the total number of undead created, and most magic items. The Tablet can be destroyed Nergal’s Beckoning produces a mass effect. All remains by sundering a powerful lawful-aligned magic item or within 1 mile of The Tablet of Chaos, starting with those weapon against it. Examples include the Fount of Law, closest in proximity and extending outward, are affected. the Aspergillum of Palantis, Caliburn, the Armature of However, the undead created by The Beckoning are not Palantis, the Spear Predestined, or an item deemed animated immediately. Rather, it is the prolonged and appropriate by the Referee. sustained exposure to The Tablet over time that calls the dead to rise. 9.narrative: The Centipede Mound (Covered): The entrance to this differently from the other mounds. The door of this mound ancient mound has been covered over with earth and is made of ancient bronze and covered with verdigris. If will need to be excavated. A stairway into this mound detected, the coverstone will radiate a strong magical descends steeply and opens into a family crypt with aura. The skull face of Nergal is embossed into the door, four stone sarcophagi. There are Giant Centipedes (6) as well as a dozen small pentagram designs. This door cannot be PCs as they enter. The sarcophagi contain the following: physically or magically breached sort of a Wish. Father: Gold Ring (40gp), Jeweled Necklace (30gp); Mother: Gold Ring (40gp), Silver Bracelet (25gp), Gold A five foot staircase descends into a rounded chamber Circlet (50gp); Daughter: Ivory Comb (25gp), Silver Ring 40x4040 feet. There are four stone columns and a pool (30gp); and the Son: Bone Handled Dagger (15gp). (10 feet by 15 feet) of oily black water in the center of the room. The water is warm and steam can be seen rising

10.narrative: Wizard’s Spire from Ironguard Motte into flour for the villagers. Hendon is a capable man (Level 4 Ranger) and occasionally This decaying spire is the home of Mazzahs the walks the moor in defense of the village. Magnificent (Level 6 Magic-User). The spire itself is impressive especially the observatory. However, the Tamson is learning the ways of the wild and apprenticing structure is in disrepair and the sides are covered in as a miller with his father. Although not yet a Level 1 green moss. A sign on the door reads “Do not disturb!” Ranger, Tamson can shoot a longbow with skill and is Mazzahs is quirky and grouchy. He prefers to be left proficient with the broadsword. If he can sneak away alone to work on his experiments, read arcane lore, and without his father knowing, he will serve as a guide for a study the cosmos through his makeshift observatory at group of PCs. Tamson is a strong lad and has 5 hit points. the top of the spire. He can also wear leather armour. Tamson’s brother Yusef dreams of adventure and wants to apprentice as a When interacting with player characters Mazzahs ranger like his father and older brother. might nod off to sleep, forget their names, name them incorrectly, or fail to remember why they came to see Vina possesses the skill to weave giant spider silk from him. The inside of his spire is significantly larger than the Blackened Forest into fine garments, cloaks, robes, or the outside might suggest. It is also very warm and cozy rope at a cost of 500gp/plus 100gp per level of the pc. despite its somewhat dilapidated condition. 15

13.narrative: Barrowmaze 12, C 9, Ch 14. Spells (2/2/2): Magic Missile, Sleep, Mazzakala’s Mucilaginous Effluvium, Light, Burning Barrowmaze, and its vast field of barrow mounds, rests a Hands, Charm Person, ESP, Magic Mouth, Pyrotechnics, half day’s march south of Helix. The first few expeditions Rope Trick, Scare, Blink, Feign Death, Gust of Wind. will require the services of a guide. Hendon’s sons can serve in this capacity. There is also a 25% chance that Mazzahs the Magnificent is an strange wizard who a hireling or henchman will know the way. The village makes his home in Helix. Mazzahs gives a poor first locals tend to be the most reliable guides. impression. His left eye is slightly larger than the right, giving him a bug-eyed appearance. His wizard cap is Referees should roll on Random Table 1: Barrowmoor bent and his robes are decorated with small charms and Random Encounters when the PCs embark for knick-knacks that jingle together as he walks. Barrowmaze and on their return to Helix. Bollo, Owner and Barkeep of The Brazen Strumpet, Level

14.title: The Bathing Crypt (Covered): The entrance to this ancient mound will need to be excavated
14.summary: The Bathing Crypt (Covered): The entrance to this ancient mound will need to be excavated. The crypt is 4 3 dry and the doors are made of wood. Entrance: A ten foot staircase descends into the entrance chamber of this crypt. All the doors in this chamber are locked. There are two pools in this room filled with dark, peat coloured water. The pools radiate magic if detected. Partially ruined frescoe…
14.narrative: The Bathing Crypt (Covered): The entrance to this ancient mound will need to be excavated. The crypt is 4 3 dry and the doors are made of wood. Entrance: A ten foot staircase descends into the entrance chamber of this crypt. All the doors in this chamber are locked. There are two pools in this room filled with dark, peat coloured water. The pools radiate magic if detected. Partially ruined frescoes on the walls depict people 2 5 1 removing their clothes and entering the pools. Disrobing and bathing in one pool reduces the intelligence of the character by one point permanently. However, bathing in both pools restores the previous point of intelligence and increases the charisma of the bather by two points (to a maximum of 18). This happens only once. 34

16.narrative: Ironguard Motte rests along a caravan route approximately 15 miles east of Helix. 17.narrative: The Ghoul Mound (Covered): The entrance to this 4 ancient mound has been covered with earth and will need to be excavated. This mound contains a stone sarcophagus. The sarcophagus has a stone mechanism at the top that appears to require a key (shaped like a scarab) to open. It cannot be opened otherwise. She is buried with a beautiful Gold Necklace (500gp), two Platinum Bracelets (400gp each), and a Dagger +1, +2 vs. Undead. The key can be found in Barrowmaze #31, Quiet Crypt #4.

Inside 2 3 1 the sarcophagus is a Ghoul (1) AL: C, AC: 6, HD: 2*, HP: 12, #AT: 3, DMG: 1d3/1d3/1d3*. She is buried with a beautiful Gold Necklace (500gp), two Platinum Bracelets (400gp each), and a Dagger +1, +2 vs. Undead. The key can be found in Barrowmaze #31, Quiet Crypt #4.

Their guts have been ripped open and fed upon. They died of battle wounds, possibly 2–3 weeks ago. One still carries a backpack with 200sp and 30gp. Burial Alcoves: (155) Square filled with urns. 19.narrative: This room smells of rot. PCs must make a Constitution check or begin vomiting for 2–5 rounds (make a random monster check). Two adventurers are impaled on the south wall with spears. Their guts have been ripped open and fed upon. They died of battle wounds, possibly 2–3 weeks ago. One still carries a backpack with 200sp and 30gp. Burial Alcoves: (155) Square filled with urns. Contents: None. A. The door to this side crypt is broken. The small crypt beyond the archway was plundered long ago. Bones and debris rest on the floor near the walls. It the PCs actively search this area they will notice passage marks on the floor but cannot ascertain by whom or what. The tracks vary considerably (mongrelmen). B. This secret room holds a small bag of 45sp in a niche on the western wall. This area also contains signs of Burial Alcoves: (51) Triangular, disturbed remains. passage that end at the wall to the north and then head Contents: 108gp. toward 23B.

23.narrative: The Barrow of Beetles (Covered): A ten foot long and a Runic Tablet. Between the doorway and the staircase descends into a round 50x50 foot mound. The crypt is in disarray. Among the rubble the PCs Barrow Mound 19 can find a runic tablet and three scroll tubes. The first is a clerical scroll with Cure Light Wounds and Cure Serious Wounds. The second is a magic-user scroll with Mazakala’s Mucilaginous Effluvium, Hitzemun’s Blue Foot, and Fennril’s Exquisite Strangulation (see New 3 Spells). Finally, there is a Protection Scroll: Ward Against Gargoyles. A small coffer can be found that contains Emeralds of varying sizes and values: 50gp, 75gp, 100gp, 100gp, 250gp, and 750gp. 2 4 1

The Acolytes of Orcus used this entrance when they first entered Barrowmaze. 1 Subsequently, this entrance has been clawed and widened by the trolls to allow them into the dungeon. 3 Troll guards are always posted at #337F at the bottom of the rough-hewn staircase. 42.narrative: The Tomb of the Acolytes (Plundered): This barrow mound was plundered long ago. The Acolytes of Orcus used this entrance when they first entered Barrowmaze. 1 Subsequently, this entrance has been clawed and widened by the trolls to allow them into the dungeon. 3 Troll guards are always posted at #337F at the bottom of the rough-hewn staircase. This is intended to provide protection while also keeping a relatively low profile near the barrow. The Acolytes will not allow this mound to be occupied and will meet resistance with maximum force. This passage and #333 (Mound #55, known only to Zur and Emnuron) are their only means into Barrowmaze,

44.narrative: Each of the six long burial passages in this large crypt holds 50 alcoves. There are four skeletons per passage that will animate and attack as soon as one of the passages is searched. Burial Alcoves: (300 total) Circular, with bones. Contents: Each passage holds 6d20cp, 4d20sp, and 1d20pp in various pouches, bags, and bowls. Four passages contain rare treasure. Passage 1 holds a clerical scroll: Cure Light Wounds(x2), Protection from Evil, and Spiritual Armor(x2) (see New Spells). Passage 3 contains a magic-user scroll: Mirror Image and Darkvision (2nd Level). The fifth passage holds a Sling +1 and the sixth passage contains a Cloak of Protection +1.

74.narrative: A number of small mausoleums line this hallway. The portcullis can be raised by a lever located at the back of one of the A. The door to this mausoleum is ajar. On the other side of the pit is a Silver Coffer sitting on B. The body of a magic-user appears to have been the lap of a skeleton in robes and covered in cobwebs. hastily pushed into this crypt. She has a small spellbook Inside the coffer is a key for the metal box located in with Sleep, Light, Read Magic, Detect Magic, and Web room #70 (Vault #5). The key detects as magical. in her backpack. Her body is in an advanced state of decay. Opening the door to this tomb will cause the character to make a Constitution check or vomit for 2–5 rounds (random monster check). C. As soon as the door to this tomb is opened a strong draft of wind will extinguish torches. Lanterns are unaffected. The tomb is empty except the skeletal remains of two humans. D. A white arrow written in chalk on the door points west with the warning, “Don’t go this way.” This mausoleum is empty. E. This mausoleum contains bones arranged on an altar. F. A trail of slime appears to extend from underneath this door, heads east for 30 feet, and then disappears. This mausoleum is empty. G. The door to this crypt is locked. It wears black Chainmail +1 and carries a Longsword +1. Note that this is a teleport location for the pit trap in room #39.

84.title: Show the players Barrowmaze illustration #15
84.summary: Show the players Barrowmaze illustration #15. There are three pools recessed into the floor. They contain liquids: black, purple, and deep red. Two silver statuettes (30gp each) sit on a low table. Quiet Crypt 2: The do…
87.narrative: A portcullis blocks the entrance to this area of the maze. Unless other methods are used, it will require the combined strength of 5 characters to lift and hold. The noise of raising the rusty portcullis will likely draw the attention of random monsters. Quiet Crypt 1: The door to this tomb is locked and the room is empty. Two silver statuettes (30gp each) sit on a low table. Quiet Crypt 2: The door to this room is ajar. One sits on a skull, atop a runic tablet, and hisses. Quiet Crypt 3: The door to this room is stuck. A hole has been chewed in the bottom. In their nest are bones and two Platinum Bracelets worth (52gp each). Frescos depict fishing and the sea. Quiet Crypt 4: This crypt is empty. Something broke free from the inside. Defiled Crypt 5: The door to this room is stuck. Frescos depict ritual sacrifice. Quiet Crypt 6: The door to this crypt is ajar. Incomprehensible script is written in charcoal on the southern wall.

A Phantom (1) AL: C, AC: N/A, HD: N/A, HP: N/A, #AT: 1, DMG: Fear, hovers in the corner of the tomb.

88.narrative: Three tomb-robbers lay dead. They managed a pretty good haul of treasure and grave goods before meeting their end. Between them there are sacks of 5,309sp, 3,980gp; as well as two white pearls: 10gp, 25gp. They also carry Potions of Undead Control (Ghast), Extra-Healing, and Growth. Alcoves along the walls include the following: Burial Alcoves: (90) Square with bone piles. Contents: One alcove contains the remains of a priest. Beside his skull rests a folded and dusty Chainmail shirt +1 and an unknown holy symbol (Zuul, the chaos deity of the elements). 80

93.title: This room is empty
93.summary: This room is empty. A broken runic tablet (now useless) rests on the floor. This room was fun to play. My players disturbed the Giant Scorpions in #94 who came to investigate. A battle ensued and quickly turned against the wounded and outnumbered party. Snake the halfling climbed the walls to safety. The hirelings Clonan and Derwood were quickly killed with the latter falling into a concealed ten …
93.narrative: This room is empty. A broken runic tablet (now useless) rests on the floor. This room was fun to play. My players disturbed the Giant Scorpions in #94 who came to investigate. A battle ensued and quickly turned against the wounded and outnumbered party. Snake the halfling climbed the walls to safety. The hirelings Clonan and Derwood were quickly killed with the latter falling into a concealed ten foot pit. Ziggy the magic-user was also wounded and in rough shape. This left Kleego the dwarf, now cornered and alone, with two options: fight the scorpions or jump into the pit. Thankfully Ziggy pulled a white rabbit out of Pinto’s Conical Cap to distract the remaining creatures. The ensuing chaos gave the party enough time to escape…barely. 182.narrative: This is a false crypt designed to trap and kill tomb- A Brief History of the Death Vault robbers. The walls are lined with alcoves with runes The Death Vault is the resting place of an elite order—The etched above each. This is merely a ruse. When the Chosen—within the faith of Nergal. first rank of PCs steps into the room, the entire floor will collapse 10 feet to a stone floor (2d6 damage, save The Chosen were fanatical followers of Nergal, led by (Death) for half). Checking for traps or using a 10 foot High Priest Rendar Serouc, and have risen in response to pole may (2 in 6) discover the trap (give a dwarf or wary the proximity of the Pit (#151) and the presence of The halfling walking in front a 3 in 6 chance to discern the Tablet of Chaos. trap). Two of the main crypt. golem’s weapons are magical. It possesses a Scimitar +1 and a Mace +1 as well as a normal flail and hand axe. The walls of the pit are sheer and difficult to climb. A fierce battle took place in this room after “Killer” Kelg Barrelgut (Level 5 Dwarf) and Cromwell (Level 5 Cleric) fell into the pit when the floor collapsed. The Bone Golem animated and attacked the two PCs. Kelg took several hits and drank two potions (Extra Healing and Growth). Cromwell tried repeatedly to cast spells and each time was struck by the many arms of the monster. The other PCs, including Sol the Bad Man (Level 3 Fighter), Snake Plisken (Level 5 Halfling), and a couple hirelings, attempted ranged attacks and fumbled around above the pit. It looked like curtains for the two adventurers until Kelg, bolstered by the potions, managed several solid hits and defeated the Bone Golem.

The alignment of the constellations appears strange and does not reflect the night sky. If studied careful…
183.narrative: The domed ceiling of this crypt is lined with diamonds that sparkle like star constellations when a torch or lantern is held aloft. One star, larger than the others, is set in the center of the dome. A concealed bottomless pit is located immediately below this star in the middle of the floor. The alignment of the constellations appears strange and does not reflect the night sky. If studied carefully for 1 turn a cleric or magic-user can interpret the stars—they portend the vengeful return of Nergal! 100

245.summary: A great set of double doors enters into this crypt. The leader of the barrow harpies, a particularly despicable female named Vultrix, has made this crypt into her personal throne room. 245.narrative: A great set of double doors enters into this crypt. The leader of the barrow harpies, a particularly despicable female named Vultrix, has made this crypt into her personal throne room. She prefers to lie in a semi-reclined position atop the sarcophagus in the eastern portion of the room. She wields a Longsword +1 Flame Tongue and a Shield +2 in battle. If the sounds of battle are heard in #244, Vultrix will send her bodyguards to investigate. She will then appear with her attendants. Large clay pots at the back of the room contain 3,853cp, 5,367sp, and 3,922ep. 117
245.stats: Vultrix (AC: 2, HD: 5, HP: 36) is stronger and more agile than the others. Vultrix is guarded by two strong male Barrow Harpies (2) AC: 4, HD: 4, HP: 28, 28 armed with tridents. There are also six female Barrow Harpies (6) HP: 19, 18, 17, 13, 12, 11 here that serve as her attendants armed with longswords and shortbows (10 arrows). There are also two Giant Vultures (2) AL: N, AC: 6, HD: 2+2, HP: 16, 13, #AT: 1, DMG: 1d6+1, that Vultrix keeps as pets.

266.narrative: This room has a ledge extending from the wall five feet from the floor. On this ledge are gargoyles of all shapes and sizes (20 total). This is the gargoyles’ first line of defense. They are all inanimate except for five (1 regular gargoyle on each wall plus a spell-casting maggog gargoyle near the door). The first two standard gargoyles will attack while the other two surprise from behind a round later. The maggog’s spells include: Magic Missile, Shield; Darkness Globe, Invisibility; Lightning Bolt. They will flee when half their number are killed.

If the other is taken it will electrocute and thereby permanently reduce the victim’s intelligence by 1 (save at –2 for no effect). This happens only once. Quiet Crypt 3: The door is locked. There are two funerary urns on a low stone altar here. There is nothing of value. Quiet Crypt 4: The door is stuck. There is nothing of value among the four skeletons interred in this crypt. Quiet Crypt 5: This door is stuck. A single sarcophagus sits in the center of this room. Inside are four funerary boxes with ash remains. There is nothing of value. Defiled Crypt 6: The door to this crypt is locked. Inside a group of evil men entombed long ago have risen as Sons of Gaxx (6) AL: C, AC: 8, HD: 3, HP: 21, 14, 14, 13, 11, 11, #AT: 1, DMG: 2d4*. They attack as soon as the door to their crypt has been opened. 133

301.narrative: The archway around the bricked-up entrance are two Decorative Amphora (800gp each) that depict displays a strange symbol (earth) alternating with a skull. ancient footraces. The bricked wall is intact and the corridor beyond is empty. Quiet Crypt 1: The door to this crypt is locked. A bowl of gemstones can be found atop a funerary box on a wooden table. The bowl contains a Small Flawed Lapis Lazuli (7gp), Small Flawed Blue Quartz (8gp), Small Blue Jasper (50gp), Small Sardonyx (100gp), Small Coral (100gp), and a Very Large Finely Cut Fire Opal (1,600gp). Defiled Crypt 2: The door to this crypt is locked. Four large Platinum Rhytons sit in stands. They are worth 1,500gp, 1,700gp, 2,000gp, and 2,200gp. Quiet Crypt 3: The door to this crypt is locked. It looks like a large green puddle on the floor. Inside a rectangular stone sarcophagus is a Valuable Scroll with unique magical properties—The Doctrine of Zuul. Clerics with neutral or chaotic alignment who read the scroll must save (Spells) or adopt Zuul as their primary deity. This can only be reversed by means of 135

Five Potions (Flying, Invisibility, set of bronze double doors (affixed with the elemental Levitation, Philter of Love, Animal Control), 4,231gp, symbol) will only open if the Key of Air is used (#298 and four Rubies of varying sizes (50gp, 250gp, 500gp, D6). The muffled sound of wind can be heard on the 500gp) can be found in a rubbish pile. other side of the door. Quiet Crypt 8: The door is locked. The crypt is empty. Inside is a large vaulted chamber 40-feet high supported Underneath a loose stone is the Key of Earth (#305). by large square columns. At the far end of the room is an altar. On the altar rests the Skull of Air with the elemental symbol engraved on its forehead. Wind swirls and gusts (#302 through #305): These four sepulchers are throughout the room from an unknown source. The gusts imbued with the chaos power of the elements. The of wind in this room make ranged attacks useless. Also, all PCs must defeat the elemental forces coalescing spells have a 50% failure rate in this room due to the wind. within each sepulcher to obtain the four Skull Keys that provide entrance into the central chamber Before the PCs can move into the room a group of Giant (#307). 136

305.title: This is the Chaos Sepulcher of Elemental Earth

The flagstone of this large chamber is either broken or covered over in places by earth, mud, and natural rock. Stalactites and stalagmites can be found throughout the room. The Skull of Earth can be found at the back of the chamber, behind the huge earth elemental.

315.title: The stone door to this crypt is sealed
315.summary: The stone door to this crypt is sealed. This is the final resting place of Luscious Revonus, a former legionnaire and politician in life. His sarcophagus stands against the west wall. His mummified remains can be found within, along with four Ivory Canopic Jars with Gold Heads (2,000gp each) representing a horse, a sphinx, a camel, and an eagle. His gladius, a Shortsword +2, can also be found at h…
315.narrative: The stone door to this crypt is sealed. This is the final resting place of Luscious Revonus, a former legionnaire and politician in life. His sarcophagus stands against the west wall. His mummified remains can be found within, along with four Ivory Canopic Jars with Gold Heads (2,000gp each) representing a horse, a sphinx, a camel, and an eagle. His gladius, a Shortsword +2, can also be found at his side.
315.desc_full: The stone door to this crypt is sealed. This is the final resting place of Luscious Revonus, a former legionnaire and politician in life. His sarcophagus stands against the west wall. His mummified remains can be found within, along with four Ivory Canopic Jars with Gold Heads (2,000gp each) representing a horse, a sphinx, a camel, and an eagle. His gladius, a Shortsword +2, can also be found at his side.

332.narrative: This is the living quarters of Zur the Black. His chambers suggest that Zur enjoys his amenities. His bed is plush with rich furs (2,400gp). The room is lit with Everburning Candles (20), and there is a small shrine to Orcus for private prayer. There is a bookstack along the east wall in front of the secret door. If the book Goat Sacrifices and Their Meaning is pulled, the floor will rotate until the bookstack turns perpendicular to the wall. Among the many books is a Manual of Golems (Runic Golem). Three runic tablets are also wedged onto the shelf. 369.narrative: The stone door to this room can only be opened by the lever in #359. When opened Minos, a huge minotaur, held in stasis, will be released. He is larger, stronger, and possesses a bronze chest plate and an over-sized Battle Axe +2 that scores 1d12 points of damage (may only be wielded by large size creatures). Minos is the Key-Master of Nergal. A great black metal key hangs around his neck. The key is magical and glows dark purple. It is the only way to open #375 (now the lair of Ossithrax). If Minos is killed, and the Tablet of Chaos has not been destroyed, he will rise in 1d4 days as a ghast and seek his revenge on the PCs.
